channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump tells GOP he wants to write 100 executive orders
item.description: “He threw that out — 100 — there could be like 100 EOs, yeah. I believe him.” Sen. Kevin Cramer (R-N.D.) told The Hill, referring to Trump.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Michelle Obama 'sends her thoughts and prayers' while absent from Carter's funeral
item.description: Michelle Obama wasn't among the mourners attending former President Carter's funeral service in Washington, but she says she's thinking of and praying for "everyone who loved and learned" from the late commander in chief.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Jimmy Carter lauded for his humility and service in Washington before his return to Georgia
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Jimmy Carter was celebrated Thursday for his personal humility and public service before, during and after his presidency during a funeral at Washington National Cathedral featuring the kind of pageantry the 39th U.S. president typically eschewed.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Colorado TikTok influencers give their thoughts about the possible ban
item.description: Munoz states, “I think it's really important for legislators, whether that be local, and then, in this case, federal legislators, to understand the complications that banning TikTok would bring to local economies.
item.dc:creator: Kyle Kawamoto, KREX 5

item.title: Jean Smart calls on TV networks to not air award shows, instead donate to Los Angeles wildfire relief
item.description: Jean Smart is urging TV networks to refrain from airing glitzy awards shows amid the devastating wildfires in the Los Angeles area, and to donate money to relief efforts instead.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Watchdog groups seeks investigation into RFK Jr. for voting from a 'sham' address
item.description: ALBANY, N.Y. (AP) — New York election officials are being urged to investigate whether Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL HILL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How to donate to LA wildfire relief
item.description: A swarm of winter wildfires ravaged America's most heavily-populated county to start 2025, scorching cars, banks, bungalows and multi-million dollar mansions.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, KRON4

item.title: Virginia mother shocked by burial of murdered daughter
item.description: Saunders sent photos of holes in the dirt on the gravesite, and one of them exposed the corner of her daughter's purple casket.
item.dc:creator: Chris Horne, NewsNation

item.title: From Carter's funeral, an American snapshot: A singular image, 5 presidents, 379 years of history
item.description: Look at their faces — formal, etched with experience, laden with the weight of momentous decisions. Add up their years: 379 to be exact, enough time to take you back into the mid-1600s, when the notion of the American nation was still more than a century away.
item.dc:creator: TED ANTHONY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Risk not 'high enough' to warrant EPA cleanup in Tijuana River Valley
item.description: "The reality is that things have gotten much worse since then," she said. "Absence of evidence is not evidence of absence.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: Schools cancel classes across parts of the US South as another burst of winter storms move in
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — Snow and icy conditions threatened to paralyze parts of the U.S. South from Texas to Alabama on Thursday, as officials closed schools, canceled or delayed flights and warned residents in some of the worst-hit areas to stay off roads as a winter storm moves east.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE and SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US warns of risk from most of world’s online pharmacies
item.description: The office of the U.S. Trade Representative warned that there’s a risk of ineffective or dangerous drugs from online pharmacies that are being illegally run.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: 4 in 10 companies planning job cuts due to AI: Survey
item.description: The survey also found that 77 percent of employers said they will adopt the strategy of “reskilling and upskilling.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Wildfires latest: At least 180,000 residents under evacuation orders in Los Angeles area
item.description: Firefighters battled early Thursday to control a series of major fires in the Los Angeles area that have killed five people, ravaged communities from the Pacific Coast to Pasadena and sent thousands of people frantically fleeing their homes.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New Mexico is a go-to state for women seeking abortions. A new court ruling helps it stay that way
item.description: The New Mexico Supreme Court has struck down abortion restrictions by conservative cities and counties at the request of the state attorney general.
item.dc:creator: Morgan Lee, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Cause of raging Los Angeles wildfires still undetermined as Mayor Karen Bass defends her leadership
item.description: Los Angeles officials delivered an update on the efforts to combat wildfires across the city on Thursday, saying more evacuations are expected.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Photos capture wildfires ravaging Southern California
item.description: All around Los Angeles, scenes of flames were everywhere, as many areas continued to burn all but unabated.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: How to safely evacuate a wildfire
item.description: Evacuating a wildfire area can be scary, but experts say people can significantly improve their own safety with a little preparation and common sense.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Gingrich predicts Johnson will be Speaker for 6 years
item.description: Former Speaker Newt Gingrich (R-Ga.) is predicting that Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) will remain in the top job for six years.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: ICE looking to expand migrant detention facilities across country, ACLU says
item.description: The American Civil Liberties Union says information it has received from U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement indicates that several migrant detention facilities on the South Texas border are among areas where the agency are seeking information to possibly expand under the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Palisades fire in LA ignites looting fears and pushes private security guards to the brink
item.description: Los Angeles DA vows to crack down on opportunist thieves amid looting as firefighters struggle to contain multiple wildfires raging in Southern California.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: California man helps father-in-law, 83, flee wildfire with walker -- with aid from 2 good Samaritans
item.description: Stories or survival are surfacing as fires continue impacting the Los Angeles area by five wildfires. Aaron Samson filmed his escape with his 83-year-old father-in-law.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Indiana man killed girlfriend's 4-month-old puppy: Docs
item.description: An Indiana man faces a felony charge after police say he threw his girlfriend's dog onto the ground in frustration, killing the 4-month-old puppy.
item.dc:creator: Joe Schroeder, KRON4

item.title: Hollywood Hills fire breaks out as deadly wildfires burn out of control across Los Angeles area
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A fast-moving fire broke out in the Hollywood Hills on Wednesday night, threatening one of Los Angeles' most iconic spots as firefighters battled to get under control three other major blazes that killed five people, put 130,000 people under evacuation orders and ravaged communiti
item.dc:creator: MANUEL VALDES, HALLIE GOLDEN and JULIE WATSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Stowaways on planes and inside landing gear raise worries about aviation security
item.description: What is going on with aviation security? People have been found dead hiding in the wheel wells of planes twice in the past month. Two stowaways were arrested on different flights in November and December. Then a passenger opened an emergency door while a plane was taxiing in Boston Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: WATCH: Reporter douses flames endangering California home on live TV
item.description: While covering one of the multiple fires currently ablaze in California, one reporter — a former EMT and volunteer firefighter — stepped in to stop flames from spreading to a home.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Jimmy Carter's life intersected with slavery's legacy. His record on Civil Rights is complicated
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Young Jimmy Carter and his friends were walking across a pasture after a day’s farm labor during the Great Depression. As they came to a gate, his companions stood aside and let Carter enter first. This was no act of kindness or intuitive deference to a future U.S. president.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: High-profile real estate brother misses hearing after mix-up in identical twins' sex trafficking case
item.description: One of three wealthy brothers accused of drugging and raping dozens of women in multiple states reportedly missed his court hearing on Tuesday after a mix-up in the case.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Is RadioShack back? Brand attempts return to relevance with consumer products
item.description: Under new leadership, RadioShack is attempting to rebuild its reputation by selling consumer electronics.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Palmer, Fox 5

item.title: Fierce overnight winds expected to fuel Pacific Palisades fire
item.description: “We looked across and the fire had jumped from one side of the road to the other side of the road,” Resident Kelsey Trainor said. “People were getting out of the cars with their dogs and babies and bags, they were crying and screaming. The road was just blocked, like full-on blocked for an hour.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, KRON4

item.title: Payments go out in $100M Verizon settlement, but people are disappointed
item.description: Payments have finally started going out in the $100 million class action lawsuit Verizon agreed to settle, but the deposits are falling short of people's expectations.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Girl Scouts getting rid of 2 cookie offerings
item.description: The Girl Scouts of the USA have announced plans to discontinue two of their “beloved” cookie offerings after the current cookie season, which kicked off Tuesday. The organization revealed the news in a press release issued that morning.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KTLA 5

item.title: Getty Images and Shutterstock to merge to become $3.7 billion visual content company
item.description: Visual content companies Shutterstock and Getty Images will join to become a $3.7 billion visual content company.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
